notes: OpenXR Loader failed to initialize
crashes steamvr (beta and stable) while compiling shaders. Tried "recommended" proton, experimental and proton GE (with and without dxvk_async=1) but all crashed

Similar to an issue present on Windows machines, the game will not get past one of two texts displaying either "OpenXR loader failed to initialize" or "Active XR Loader Open XR Loader".

The first message displays when running the linux beta build of SteamVR; swapping to the most recent allows the game to properly hook at the expense of the dashboard and settings menus.

The second message displays every time I load the game in the most recent release of SteamVR. Unsure why, but the game hangs on this message every time.

Steam Play settings use Proton Experimental as the default compatibility layer, but setting the game's compatibility layer individually to Proton Experimental causes it to crash the moment the second message loads. At a glance, using GE Proton 7-35 seems to yield the same results as Experimental (correction: using GE Proton 7-35 seems to cause a crash in SteamVR trying to load the game; otherwise, the results are the same).

Hoping to change this contribution from a Borked! to a Silver once some patches roll out. 🤞

Game freezes upon loading at the OpenXR splash screen. Often either crashing SteamVR completely (black display and an error message on SteamVR) or causing the entire graphics stack to freeze and thus require a reset of the entire system.
Solid experience with SteamVR [beta] on release day

steamvr settings > advanced > developer > set SteamVR as Open XR loader 
Still borked though after doing that. Freezes at "Active Loader Open XR Loader" and then crashes headset/SteamVR

Game good, lavagang forever

I've tried 3 different versions of SteamVR:
- Standard build
- Beta build
- Legacy "linux_v1.14" build
Unfortunately none seem to work. 

Using the legacy build does have the game prompt that OpenXR fails to load. Likely doesn't have OpenXR support. Honestly surprised that it doesn't work at all, considering it's a sequel to Boneworks which I've had no issues running previously I'd thought they would be similar enough to at least playable.

notes:
Needed to set to steamvr beta
Also steam vr prombted to set open xr loader as default.

First I got past the "Failed to initialize OpenXR" message by setting SteamVR as the OpenXR driver. Go to SteamVR Settings -> Developer Settings to do this.
But even after setting SteamVR as OpenXR driver, the game crashes and then freezes my system, forcing hard reboot. I can prevent it from freezing my system by running steam through konsole (kde terminal) instead of launching it from my desktop environment. Doing this the game just crashes without freezing my system and then launches a small window which I assume to be an automated crash reporting tool from the game devs. I also tried switching to SteamVR Linux beta client but that didn't work.
Doesn't work yet, I REALLY hope that it'll work in the future.
Once the game gets to "Active OpenXR runtime"  X11 freezes for a few seconds and steamvr crashes. Appears to be a Nvidia only issue based on all the working reports being AMD.
Same OpenXR Loader + -203 error everyone else with an Nvidia card gets

I've tried Proton versions 7.04, Forced Experimental, and Glorious Eggroll 7.20 iirc. The former didn't work, and the latter two did, but I couldn't get past the G114 Void loading screen, as mentioned before. alx pls fix

Attempted to open this game via my Valve Index headset only to have the headset shut off as the loading screen popped up. I went over to my computer and while the cursor could move around the loading screen, nothing else could be done. No run prompt, alt tab, terminal, etc. I had to hard power off because pressing the power button to sleep didn't even work. Upon rebooting I tried it again, but this time launched it from Steam without VR, and the same exact thing happened. My entire system crashed on the splash screen that says inivializing VR something something. I'll be returning this game immediately.
